entity.summary: BNB Smart Chain decentralized exchange, yield optimizer, and launchpad ecosystem. It suffered an economic exploit in May 2021 and announced the end of all platform development in March 2022.
entity.notes: Launch date is normalized to 2021-04-01 because available exchange references establish a 2021 start and first-party material shows public operation by May, but an exact launch day was not recovered. The terminal date is exact: the team announced on 2022-03-04 that all development would stop and remaining treasury and liquidity would be redistributed or left for withdrawal. The former official domain now serves unrelated gambling content and is classified unsafe; residual third-party metrics do not override the first-party shutdown announcement.

events.title: AutoShark entered public operation in 2021
events.description: AutoShark began operating on BNB Smart Chain as a decentralized exchange and yield-optimization ecosystem.
events.notes: The day is normalized from the documented 2021 launch year and first-party operation before the May exploit.

events.title: AutoShark suffered an economic exploit
events.description: An attacker used an economic and flash-loan manipulation path that collapsed the SHARK token price and extracted roughly 2,500 BNB from the protocol ecosystem.
events.notes: The first-party post explains the price manipulation and immediate response; contemporary reporting provides the approximate loss figure.

events.title: AutoShark stopped all platform development
events.description: The AutoShark team announced that it would stop all development, disable further minting where applicable, and redistribute or leave remaining protocol assets for community withdrawal.
events.notes: HEI treats the announcement as the terminal platform date because it explicitly ended development and described final asset distribution.

evidence.title: AutoShark Finance exchange profile
evidence.notes: Historical exchange profile identifies AutoShark Finance as a decentralized exchange established in 2021.

evidence.title: How PancakeBunny got compromised and AutoShark's preventive measures
evidence.notes: First-party publication confirms active protocol operation before the May 2021 exploit.

evidence.title: How AutoShark got economically exploited
evidence.notes: First-party incident report explains the economic exploit and the collapse of SHARK from about $1.20 to $0.01.

evidence.title: AutoShark Finance exploited for 2,500 BNB
evidence.notes: Contemporary report corroborates the exploit date and approximate 2,500 BNB impact.

evidence.title: End of an era
evidence.notes: First-party terminal announcement states that all AutoShark platform development would stop and describes the final withdrawal and redistribution process.

evidence.title: AutoShark former official domain repurposed
evidence.notes: The former official project domain currently serves unrelated gambling content and must not be exposed as a safe current exchange destination.
